title: my plan EVACON 2015
wikitext:
[]: my plan: EVACON 2015             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:25 pm lately i've found out that most people on this website are from maryland, so this has hardened my resolve to use this idea i have had since march. me and som)e friends are going to save money for a while, then in 2015 we are going to rent a hotel ballroom and hold an eva conevantion we will call EVACON. it will probably be a one shot thing, and we'll do it in 2015 for obvious reasons. we plan to show the entire eva TV series, all the movies (yes, even rebuild as much as i despise it.), we'll do cosplay, present artwork, rare collectibles, read fan fiction, listen to music, debate, and more. id like to see though first the reaction i get from this and see if there are any volunteers to present something. (we don't want money though, theres no honor in doing fufilling your visions with the donations of others.) i was thinking cosplayers to do skits, mabey some poets. and im open to ideas. so lets discuss the concept of EVACON. note: i for one am going to sing "Fly me to the Moon"

[]: Re: my plan: EVACON 2015             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:28 pm shinjidoggysasuka wrote: we'll do it in 2015 for obvious reasons.

[]: Re: my plan: EVACON 2015             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:33 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: shinjidoggysasuka wrote: we'll do it in 2015 for obvious reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by knightrous » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:34 pm In all honesty, I think this could be something epic. 6 years is a lot of time for preparation, I'll be keeping my eye on this ;)

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:35 pm knightrous wrote: In all honesty, I think this could be something epic. 6 years is a lot of time for preparation, I'll be keeping my eye on this ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:37 pm Maybe this thread would work better in the Fandom Area...?

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Tue Nov 17, 2009 6:39 pm Joseph the PRPD wrote: Maybe this thread would work better in the Fandom Area...?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 17, 2009 7:31 pm I started sketching out ideas for an "Evacon" slated for 2015 around August 2005. Naturally, I thought to myself, "2015 is the perfect year to do it, but I shouldn't think about seriously until around 2012". My notes included plans for guests, video programming, live programming, main events, fan parodies, and so forth. In other words, I'm way ahead of you on this. But if you leave it up to me, it will never happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Tue Nov 17, 2009 10:13 pm You're not the only one to think about making EvaCon - I myself have had the idea in my head since '07, before I ever knew about this place. Maybe you, me, and Eva Monkey can pitch in a little as far as things like events and design are concerned (though have a good amount of cash on hand in case neither of us can help in that area). IT'LL BE FUN!

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Nov 17, 2009 10:48 pm Hey in 2015 I will be one year from graduating college. I'm sure there will be some convention having a special section about Eva, if not entirely about it.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Tue Nov 17, 2009 11:11 pm Having a major Evangelion event in 2015 is a good idea. I bet Gainax is already working on it. Start booking your tickets for Tokyo now.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Tue Nov 17, 2009 11:39 pm You guys are forgetting that 2015 will never come. The world is obviously going to end in 2012. Duh.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Nov 18, 2009 12:10 am I think I will be too busy in 2015 to care about most animes. And why plan something six years from now?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Nov 18, 2009 12:26 am @schiss: Noone knows when the world will end. People thought it would end in like Y2K, or in he 90's or some shit like that. But nobody knows the year, the day, the hour or the moment that the rapture will take place and the antichrist will rise and the world will end. (Forgive me for bringing my own religion into this ) And as far as people not caring about anime in their later years I know like 30-40+ year olds who still enjoy stuff like anime or roleplaying.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Nov 18, 2009 1:12 am I think this is a really good idea, so much so that in 2015 I might be willing to pay a visit to the US to attend. You should probably collaborate with EM and any other higher ups that know their stuff in order to make it a success though. I reckon that if it is done correctly, it could be really big, and not just for evageeks, but for the entire Evangelion community.

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Nov 18, 2009 11:37 am Great Genius Shinji-Sama wrote: @schiss: Noone knows when the world will end. People thought it would end in like Y2K, or in he 90's or some shit like that. But nobody knows the year, the day, the hour or the moment that the rapture will take place and the antichrist will rise and the world will end. (Forgive me for bringing my own religion into this ) And as far as people not caring about anime in their later years I know like 30-40+ year olds who still enjoy stuff like anime or roleplaying.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Nov 18, 2009 11:46 am Shouldn't this be in Neo-Tokyo 3?

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Nov 18, 2009 1:05 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I started sketching out ideas for an "Evacon" slated for 2015 around August 2005. Naturally, I thought to myself, "2015 is the perfect year to do it, but I shouldn't think about seriously until around 2012". My notes included plans for guests, video programming, live programming, main events, fan parodies, and so forth. In other words, I'm way ahead of you on this. But if you leave it up to me, it will never happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:00 pm In six years, I'll probably be married (to my current girlfriend, most likely), and will undoubtedly drag her and possibly Lil' Veg along for the ride, though I'm pretty sure that said GF will be a little upset about having to attend an Evangelion convention (she thinks that Eva is stupid and boring, an opinion I haven't been able to change), but will still support me anyway and come along - though she'll have to find something nice to wear (I'm still coming dressed up as Geese Howard, so no Eva-cosplay Veg for you).

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Nov 18, 2009 6:08 pm Vegeta 20XX wrote: In six years, I'll probably be married (to my current girlfriend, most likely), and will undoubtedly drag her and possibly Lil' Veg along for the ride, though I'm pretty sure that said GF will be a little upset about having to attend an Evangelion convention (she thinks that Eva is stupid and boring, an opinion I haven't been able to change), but will still support me anyway and come along - though she'll have to find something nice to wear (I'm still coming dressed up as Geese Howard, so no Eva-cosplay Veg for you).
